title: Lepechiniella saurica
taxonomy: Lepechiniella saurica is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Boraginaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as borage or forget-me-not. It belongs to the genus Lepechiniella, a group of flowering plants that are native to the Mediterranean region. The species Lepechiniella saurica is a shrub-like plant that is characterized by its small, white flowers and narrow leaves. Lepechiniella saurica is also classified as a member of the order Boraginales, which is a group of flowering plants that are known for their unique flower structures. Overall, the classification of Lepechiniella saurica reflects its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

preparations: Lepechiniella saurica, a plant native to Western Australia, has been used in traditional medicine by the indigenous people.
